title: Kiss all the Time. Disco, Occasionally
notes: A great example of individually good songs making a great album, with a cohesive story about someone falling in love. The contrast between upbeat and harrowing lyrics keeps it replayable. There is quiet musicianship with intentional details throughout.
highlights: *Coming Up Roses* and *American Girls* are definite standouts. The strings in *Coming Up Roses* are impressively written with an evocative bridge. *American Girls* showcases classic Harry Styles with tastefully produced synth and is extremely catchy. His trademark harmony style is excellent.
lowlights: Some songs like *Taste Back*, *Waiting Game*, and *Carla's Songs* feel a bit mundane though still solid individually. Wish there was more funk and groove throughout the album.
